verses.1: A false balance is an abomination to the LORD,
verses.2: When pride comes, then comes shame,
verses.3: The integrity of the upright shall guide them,
verses.4: Riches don’t profit in the day of wrath,
verses.5: The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way,
verses.6: The righteousness of the upright shall deliver them,
verses.7: When a wicked man dies, hope perishes,
verses.8: A righteous person is delivered out of trouble,
verses.9: With his mouth the godless man destroys his neighbor,
verses.10: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices.
verses.11: By the blessing of the upright, the city is exalted,
verses.12: One who despises his neighbor is void of wisdom,
verses.13: One who brings gossip betrays a confidence,
verses.14: Where there is no wise guidance, the nation falls,
verses.15: He who is collateral for a stranger will suffer for it,
verses.17: The merciful man does good to his own soul,
verses.19: He who is truly righteous gets life.
verses.20: Those who are perverse in heart are an abomination to the LORD,
verses.21: Most certainly, the evil man will not be unpunished,
verses.22: Like a gold ring in a pig’s snout,
verses.23: The desire of the righteous is only good.
verses.24: There is one who scatters, and increases yet more.
verses.25: The liberal soul shall be made fat.
verses.26: People curse someone who withholds grain,
verses.27: He who diligently seeks good seeks favor,
verses.28: He who trusts in his riches will fall,
verses.29: He who troubles his own house shall inherit the wind.
verses.30: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life.
verses.31: Behold, the righteous shall be repaid in the earth,
